title: Elaphoglossum callolepis
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum callolepis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is classified under the genus Elaphoglossum and the order Polypodiales. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly known as wood ferns or shield ferns. Elaphoglossum callolepis is a small, terrestrial fern that is native to Central and South America. Its classification as a species of Elaphoglossum places it within the larger group of leptosporangiate ferns.

intro: Elaphoglossum callolepis is a species of fern, specifically a type of epiphytic fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, Elaphoglossum callolepis has been used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the management of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
